researchers.name: Kenrick et al.
researchers.spec_role: Renovating Pyramid of Needs (most structurally similar precedent)

researchers.name: Liebig
researchers.spec_role: DA9 historical precedent — Law of the Minimum
researchers.what_we_take: Structural form of the Law of the Minimum: system performance is bounded by the scarcest essential input, not the average. Cor adopts this structural form for mechanism resolution; the agronomy content does not transfer.

works.title: Die organische Chemie in ihrer Anwendung auf Agricultur und Physiologie

researchers.name: Bullmore (Edward)
researchers.spec_role: Psychiatrist and neuroscientist. Head of Department of Psychiatry at University of Cambridge and co-leader of GlaxoSmithKline clinical-imaging division. Primary researcher on neuroimmunology of depression — the cytokine / inflammation / mood cascade. His 2018 book The Inflamed Mind synthesizes the Dantzer/Raison/Miller/Danese literature on cytokine sickness behavior, inflammation as causal in depression, and the failed monoamine paradigm for a general audience.
researchers.what_we_take: Bullmore's synthesis of the cytokine sickness-behavior cascade (IL-1b, TNF-alpha, IL-6 as mediators of depression-like states), the inflammation-depression causal link (not just correlation), the systematic failure of the serotonin-hypothesis monoamine paradigm, and the stress->immune->mood causal chain. Load-bearing for M8 Immune Regulation at the synthesis level, with supporting contributions to M3 Social Bonding (inflammation-social-withdrawal link) and M6 Controllability/Agency (sickness behavior as evolved withdrawal).
researchers.what_we_dont_take: Bullmore's framing presents the inflammation-depression causal link as more settled than the primary literature fully supports. Some of his specific claims about anti-inflammatory treatments for depression are optimistic projections rather than replicated clinical findings. His dismissal of the serotonin paradigm is broadly correct but glosses the remaining evidence for serotonergic modulation in specific depression subtypes.

works.title: The Inflamed Mind: A Radical New Approach to Depression

researchers.name: Cacioppo (John T.)
researchers.spec_role: Loneliness as evolved signaling mechanism; social neuroscience; UCLA Loneliness Scale lineage and Ohio State / University of Chicago lab program on physiological consequences of perceived social isolation. Secondary role: Hawkley-Cacioppo hypervigilance-to-threat cascade in lonely individuals.
researchers.what_we_take: Loneliness as an aversive signal selected to motivate re-connection (parallel to hunger/thirst/pain); the social-homeostasis framing; the hypervigilance-to-threat cascade; cortisol dawn rise, elevated TPR under stress, and dampened ventral-striatum reward response to social stimuli as physiological consequences of perceived isolation.
researchers.what_we_dont_take: Do not extrapolate from Cacioppo's cardiovascular/cortisol findings in Ohio State / Chicago undergraduate samples to clinical populations without downgrading; some specific findings (TPR direction, gene-expression magnitudes) are from single samples awaiting broader replication.

works.title: Loneliness: Human Nature and the Need for Social Connection

researchers.name: Field (Tiffany)
researchers.spec_role: Developmental psychologist and director of the Touch Research Institute at University of Miami Miller School of Medicine. Primary researcher for R1 Touch. Her work established touch as a measurable developmental and therapeutic input with quantifiable effects on cortisol, weight gain (premature infants), growth, pain, and behavior. Field has published hundreds of papers on massage therapy, touch deprivation, maternal-infant touch, and cultural variation in touch.
researchers.institution: Touch Research Institute, University of Miami Miller School of Medicine
researchers.what_we_take: Field's empirical demonstrations of touch as a regulatory input: the premature-infant massage weight-gain finding, cortisol reduction across clinical populations, and the broader case that touch deprivation produces measurable developmental and regulatory deficits. Her synthesis of Harlow, Jourard, Montagu, and the Romanian orphanage literature as the comparative frame for understanding touch as a phylogenetically ancient input distinct from vision and hearing.
researchers.what_we_dont_take: Some of Field's clinical massage claims rest on small-sample Touch Research Institute studies with variable replication outside her lab. The dose-response specifics (minutes of touch needed, specific pressure/temperature parameters) are often extrapolated from limited data. The 'touch hunger' framing is compelling but operationalization varies across her publications.

works.title: Touch

researchers.name: Fisher (Helen)
researchers.spec_role: Reproductive motivation architecture; three-component system (lust / romantic love / attachment) with distinct neurochemical substrates; fMRI studies of romantic love with Lucy Brown and Art Aron; cross-cultural fieldwork on pair-bonding, mating, divorce, and adultery; Match.com singles-survey data on contemporary mating patterns. Listed in M14 the M14 source-researchers list.
researchers.what_we_take: The three-component architecture of reproductive motivation (lust/testosterone, romantic love/dopamine, attachment/oxytocin-vasopressin); the VTA-reward-system localization of romantic love; cross-species pair-bonding evidence (prairie vole dopamine, vasopressin, MHC findings); cross-cultural universality of romantic love and pair-bonding; the evolutionary logic for sex-differentiated mate strategies.
researchers.what_we_dont_take: Some of Fisher's neurochemical correspondences are stronger than the underlying data support (the specific lust=testosterone/love=dopamine/attachment=oxytocin mapping is a heuristic simplification of a much more interactive reality). Fisher's popular-audience framing sometimes treats correlational neuroimaging findings as causal. The Match.com survey data are convenience samples, not representative populations.

works.title: Anatomy of Love: A Natural History of Mating, Marriage, and Why We Stray

researchers.name: Haugen (Frances)
researchers.spec_role: DC2 — attention-economy platforms as engineered proxy exploitation
researchers.what_we_take: Whistleblower documentation (The Facebook Files, 2021) showing internal company knowledge that engagement-maximizing ranking algorithms harm users — particularly adolescent girls via Instagram social-comparison effects on M5 — while the market incentive structure prevented correction. Empirical anchor for the DC2 claim that market incentives systematically push toward proxy exploitation.

works.title: Social Relationships and Mortality Risk: A Meta-analytic Review

researchers.name: Lieberman (Daniel)
researchers.spec_role: Evolutionary anthropologist and human-evolutionary biologist. Primary scholar of the evolutionary biology of physical activity, running, walking, and the mismatch between evolved movement patterns and modern sedentary lifestyles. Named in M10 Movement/Regulatory source_researchers array. His work on hunter-gatherer activity levels, endurance-running evolution, and the active grandparent hypothesis provides the central evolutionary framing for M10 and M13.
researchers.institution: Harvard University (Edwin M. Lerner II Professor of Biological Sciences)
researchers.what_we_take: The active-grandparent hypothesis (longevity selected for physical activity in older humans); the evolutionary framing of daily movement as embedded in survival rather than as discretionary exercise; the cross-cultural comparison showing hunter-gatherers are only ~15% more active than Westerners who exercise an hour daily; the debunking of 'we evolved to exercise' and 'sitting is the new smoking' as folk category errors; the emphasis on physical activity shaping health span more than life span.
researchers.what_we_dont_take: Some of Lieberman's evolutionary hypotheses (especially the endurance-running / persistence-hunting account of human bipedality) are still contested in evolutionary anthropology and should be treated as theoretical rather than established. His popular-audience framing sometimes glosses the limits of Hadza data as a proxy for ancestral hunter-gatherer populations. His account of the Pontzer constrained-energy-expenditure hypothesis presents it as more settled than the literature currently supports.

works.title: Exercised: The Science of Physical Activity, Rest and Health
The Story of the Human Body: Evolution, Health, and Disease
The Status Syndrome: How Social Standing Affects Our Health and Longevity
Epigenetic programming by maternal behavior

researchers.name: Moss (Michael)
researchers.spec_role: DC2 — food industry market proxy exploitation
researchers.what_we_take: Investigative documentation of how processed food companies engineered products to maximize palatability via salt/sugar/fat bliss points, deliberately bypassing satiety regulation (M13) to drive consumption and profit. Landmark: 'Salt Sugar Fat: How the Food Giants Hooked Us' (2013).

works.title: Salt Sugar Fat

researchers.name: Muldoon & Park
researchers.spec_role: DC2 — companion AI as market proxy exploitation (IDENTITY UNVERIFIED — see caveat)
researchers.what_we_take: Cited in HANDOFF_COR_V2_REBUILD.md DC2 derivation line as empirical evidence on companion AI as engineered proxy exploitation of M3 (social bonding). Exact publication and institutional identity not confirmed from the handoff text alone. Corherence flagged: if this should be resolved to specific researchers (e.g., Muldoon at Institute for Future Studies, Park at a companion AI research group), the entry can be updated.

researchers.name: Pellis (Sergio & Vivien)
researchers.spec_role: Comparative psychologists at the University of Lethbridge. Primary research on the neurobiology and cross-species behavioral ecology of play, particularly play-fighting in rats and across mammalian taxa. Named in M4 Social Calibration/Play source_researchers array. Their work provides the definitive synthesis of the neurobiological substrates of play (prefrontal cortex, amygdala, somatosensory cortex, opioid/dopamine modulation) and the cross-species comparative study of play behavior.
researchers.what_we_take: The prefrontal-cortex role in calibrating play bouts; play-deprivation consequences on social competence and stress regulation; the taxonomic distribution of play across mammals (and the absence in some lineages); play fighting as a real behavior distinct from actual aggression; the neural substrates of play behavior and their interaction with the SEEKING and PLAY circuits identified by Panksepp. Their empirical work is the load-bearing evidence for M4 at the neurobiological and comparative levels.
researchers.what_we_dont_take: Some of Pellis & Pellis's generalizations from rat play-fighting to human social calibration are theoretical extrapolations rather than directly tested claims. Their cross-species taxonomic comparisons sometimes conflate homologous and convergent play behaviors.

works.title: The Playful Brain: Venturing to the Limits of Neuroscience

researchers.name: Pontzer (Herman)
researchers.spec_role: Evolutionary anthropologist and human energetics researcher. Primary researcher on the constrained total energy expenditure model — the finding that active populations (hunter-gatherers, subsistence farmers, physically demanding jobs) have total daily energy expenditure similar to sedentary industrialized populations of the same body size, because the body adapts metabolically to keep TEE within a narrow range. His Hadza field studies and multi-population comparisons are the empirical backbone for M13 Energy Regulation.
researchers.institution: Duke University (Department of Evolutionary Anthropology; previously Hunter College)
researchers.what_we_take: The constrained total energy expenditure finding itself (multi-population cross-sectional doubly-labeled-water evidence), the specific population-comparison data (Hadza, Tsimane, US, Ghana, Jamaica, South Africa), and the downstream implication that exercise does not produce linear caloric deficits. Also the evolutionary framing of TEE as a species-typical trait homeostatically buffered against habitual activity variation.
researchers.what_we_dont_take: The specific mechanism underlying activity energy expenditure 2 (non-muscular compensation) remains hypothesis rather than demonstrated — Pontzer is honest about this in the 2016 paper. Cross-sectional design limitation (no longitudinal within-subject test) means the causal direction of the compensation remains inferred. Some of the public-health implications Pontzer draws (exercise is ineffective for weight loss) are stronger than the core finding strictly supports.

works.title: Hunter-Gatherer Energetics and Human Obesity
Constrained Total Energy Expenditure and Metabolic Adaptation to Physical Activity in Adult Humans
Sitting, Squatting, and the Evolutionary Biology of Human Inactivity

researchers.name: Schüll (Natasha Dow)
researchers.spec_role: DC2 — gambling machine design as engineered proxy exploitation
researchers.what_we_take: Ethnographic and design-analytic documentation of how modern slot machines are deliberately engineered for variable-reward open-loop stimulation of SEEKING/M2 without resolution, producing 'machine zone' dissociative states and compulsive play. Landmark: 'Addiction by Design: Machine Gambling in Las Vegas' (2012).

works.title: Addiction by Design

researchers.name: Boyd
researchers.what_we_take: gene-culture coevolution; matched must include cultural scaffolding.
researchers.what_we_dont_take: The specific formalism of dual-inheritance mathematical models is not directly imported into Cor's architecture; Cor treats the cultural-scaffolding constraint as a boundary condition on matched/mismatched rather than running the full population-dynamics machinery. Cor also treats cultural-selection mechanisms as a constraint on what counts as matched, not as a primary mechanism in the motivational-emotional architecture. The rich-cultural-transmission-variance findings (conformist bias, prestige bias, payoff bias) are acknowledged as real shapers of human development but are not themselves mechanisms in the 14+R1 inventory.

works.title: Not by Genes Alone: How Culture Transformed Human Evolution

researchers.name: Cosmides
researchers.what_we_take: Cosmides co-founded evolutionary psychology with Tooby and contributed a set of specific empirical and theoretical moves that Cor draws on directly. Her Wason selection task work demonstrates domain-specific reasoning: humans reliably solve logical problems framed as social-contract cheater detection but fail at the same formal problem in abstract form, supporting a domain-sensitive cognitive architecture rather than general-purpose reasoning. This is primary evidence for P2 (Domain-Sensitive Interacting Adaptations) and grounds the claim that cooperative reasoning has dedicated circuitry. Her computational theory of social exchange (reciprocal altruism + cheater detection as an evolved solution to the fitness problem of free-riding) also grounds M11 Cooperation/Alliance at the cognitive-architecture level.

researchers.name: Darwin
researchers.what_we_take: the loss function.

works.title: On the Origin of Species by Means of Natural Selection

works.title: The Genetical Evolution of Social Behaviour (I and II)

works.title: The Secret of Our Success: How Culture is Driving Human Evolution, Domesticating Our Species, and Making Us Smarter

researchers.what_we_dont_take: full ontological claims about what lies beneath.

works.title: Interface Theory of Perception

researchers.name: Konner
researchers.what_we_take: mismatch framework foundational.

researchers.name: Li (Norman)
researchers.what_we_take: four-step mismatch identification framework, forced vs hijacked mismatch distinction.

works.title: The Mismatch Hypothesis: What Can We Learn from Hunter-Gatherers?

researchers.name: Richerson
researchers.what_we_take: Richerson's contributions complement Boyd's and extend into specific domains Cor draws on. His work on cultural transmission dynamics — how reliable cultural information can accumulate faster than genetic evolution allows, and how conformist / prestige / payoff biases shape what gets transmitted — grounds the P3 constraint that 'matched' must include culturally-scaffolded variation, not just ancestral surface forms. His tribal social instincts hypothesis (that humans evolved psychology specifically adapted for living in symbol-marked cultural groups with moral norms) is load-bearing for M11 Cooperation/Alliance and for reading group-level norm enforcement as an evolved mechanism rather than a cultural accident. His climate-and-cultural-evolution work provides the environmental-variability argument for why cultural learning is itself an evolved adaptation.

researchers.name: Tooby
researchers.what_we_take: domain-sensitivity, EEA, computational treatment of emotions.

works.title: The Adapted Mind: Evolutionary Psychology and the Generation of Culture

researchers.name: van Vugt
